Seth Pruitt
About
Working Papers
The Empirical Virtue of Complexity in Simple Economic Models Short Selling and the Processing of Public Information Risk exposures from risk disclosures: What they said and how they said it R&R JFE Dogs and Cats Living Together: A Defense of Cash-Flow Predictability The Cost of ESG Investing Instrumented Principal Component Analysis R&R QE
Published Papers
Modeling Corporate Bond Returns JF ’23 Understanding Momentum and Reversal JFE ’21 Earnings Risk in the Household: Evidence from Millions of U.S. Tax Returns AERI ’20 Characteristics are Covariances: A Unified Model of Risk and Return JFE ’19 The Liquidity Effects of Official Bond Market Intervention JFQA ’18 Estimating Monetary Policy Rules When Interest Rates Are Stuck at Zero JMCB ’17 Systemic Risk and the Macroeconomy: An Empirical Evaluation JFE ’16 The Three-Pass Regression Filter: A New Approach to Forecasting with Many Predictors JoE ’15 The Demand for Youth: Explaining Age Differences in the Volatility of Hours AER ’13 Market Expectations in the Cross Section of Present Values JF ’13 Uncertainty over Models and Data: The Rise and Fall of American Inflation JMCB ’12 Estimating the Market-Perceived Monetary Policy Rule AEJMacro ’11
Other Articles
Dissecting Market Expectations in the Cross-Section of Book-to-Market Ratios: A Comment CFR ’22
Older Working Papers
Reconciling TRACE bond returns Markup Variation and Endogenous Fluctuations in the Price of Investment Goods The Pseudo-Information Filter
Downloads

Maintained Software

Code I actively develop and maintain.

IPCA Python
Python implementation, building off of the IPCA estimator (Kelly, Pruitt, and Su 2019).
GitHub pip install git+https://github.com/sjpsjpsjp/ipca.git

Replication Archives

Code and data for published and working papers, as released alongside each paper.
